description: Pre-built finding scenarios for mock internal audits. Each scenario has theme + severity + applicable_frameworks tags; cross-reference scripts/cross_framework_mapper.py merged-controls catalogue to resolve framework-specific control IDs.

severity_levels.critical: Major nonconformity: absence of, or systemic failure to implement, a required management-system process. Blocks certification at stage 1.
severity_levels.major: Material gap in a required control. Corrective action plan within 30 days.
severity_levels.minor: Localized gap; control works overall. Corrective action within 90 days.
severity_levels.observation: Improvement opportunity; no nonconformity. Optional recommendation.

scenarios.title: Orphaned privileged access from terminations
scenarios.description: Quarterly access review missed 3 cycles; 12 terminated employees retain prod admin access > 90 days post-termination. Audit log shows 4 of them performed actions in the system after their last working day.
scenarios.remediation: Immediate revocation; investigate access logs for unauthorized activity; reinstate quarterly review cadence with automated tooling.

scenarios.title: Shared admin credentials in production
scenarios.description: Production database admin password shared across 5 engineers; rotation last performed > 12 months ago. No audit trail for individual actions.
scenarios.remediation: Rotate immediately; provision per-user named accounts; enable individual audit logging; document procedure.

scenarios.title: Quarterly access review evidence lacks justification
scenarios.description: Quarterly access review records exist but lack documented business justification for retained privileges. Reviewers approve in bulk without per-user rationale.
scenarios.remediation: Update review template to require per-user justification; train reviewers; sample-check next quarter.

scenarios.title: JML workflow does not auto-deprovision
scenarios.description: Joiner-mover-leaver workflow exists but is manual; observed 5+ day gap between HR termination and access revocation.
scenarios.remediation: Implement IDP integration with HR system for auto-deprovisioning within 24 hours; trail for exceptions.

scenarios.title: MFA not enforced on admin accounts
scenarios.description: Multi-factor authentication is documented in policy but not technically enforced on cloud admin accounts; 8 admin users authenticate without MFA.
scenarios.remediation: Enforce MFA at IDP level; emergency-break-glass procedure documented; close legacy accounts.

scenarios.title: Access review records lack completion timestamps
scenarios.description: Access review records lack documented review-completion timestamps in 2 of 6 sampled reviews. Cannot confirm review was completed on time.
scenarios.remediation: Update review tooling to capture timestamp at review-action time; backfill where possible.

scenarios.title: RBAC matrix doesn't cover cloud resources
scenarios.description: Role-based access control matrix exists for application tier but does not address cloud-resource scope (IAM policies, S3 buckets, KMS keys).
scenarios.remediation: Extend RBAC matrix; document cloud-IAM role-to-business-role mapping.

scenarios.title: Consider just-in-time (JIT) access for production
scenarios.description: Standing access to production is the default; JIT access with approval workflow would reduce blast radius and improve audit trail.
scenarios.remediation: Pilot JIT tooling (e.g., Teleport, ConductorOne, ConsoleMe) for one team.

scenarios.title: Privileged access review cadence could be more frequent
scenarios.description: Quarterly cadence meets standard; for ≥ critical-tier systems, monthly review provides earlier detection of orphaned access.
scenarios.remediation: Increase cadence for critical-tier systems to monthly.

scenarios.title: Session timeout policies inconsistent
scenarios.description: Session-timeout policies vary across applications (30 min in CRM, 8 hours in BI tool, no timeout in internal admin tool). Inconsistent risk posture.
scenarios.remediation: Define policy by data sensitivity tier; align tooling configuration.

scenarios.title: Asset inventory missing cloud + SaaS + AI tools
scenarios.description: Asset register includes server inventory but omits 60% of SaaS tools and 100% of AI/LLM tools acquired in past 12 months. No central source of truth.
scenarios.remediation: Integrate SSO with SaaS-discovery tooling; require AI-tool registration before procurement; quarterly refresh.

scenarios.title: Data classification scheme not applied
scenarios.description: Data classification scheme documented (public / internal / confidential / restricted) but only 30% of data stores have classification labels applied.
scenarios.remediation: Apply classification to remaining stores; automate via DLP tooling where feasible.

scenarios.title: Asset owners not assigned for 15% of assets
scenarios.description: 15% of inventory entries lack named owners; orphan ownership impedes timely incident response.
scenarios.remediation: Assign owners; require owner field on new asset creation.

scenarios.title: Third-party AI services not tagged in inventory
scenarios.description: Inventory does not flag which assets are powered by third-party AI services (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere). Material for ISO 42001 A.10 + EU AI Act Article 25.
scenarios.remediation: Add AI-vendor tag; update procurement intake form.

scenarios.title: Asset decommissioning workflow informal
scenarios.description: When assets are decommissioned, data destruction is documented but inventory entries persist; clutters reporting.
scenarios.remediation: Add decommission state to inventory schema; archive after retention period.

scenarios.title: Risk register without treatment plans
scenarios.description: Risk register identifies 30+ risks but lacks documented treatment plans (modify/share/retain/avoid per ISO 23894) for high/critical risks.
scenarios.remediation: Run risk-treatment workshop per high/critical risk; document treatment + signoff; link to specific controls.

scenarios.title: AI risk assessment not re-run after material model change
scenarios.description: AI risk assessment last performed at initial deployment 18 months ago. Model has been retrained twice; risk profile not re-evaluated.
scenarios.remediation: Trigger re-assessment; update register; document drift monitoring threshold; commit to re-assessment on every material change.

scenarios.title: Risk methodology inconsistently applied
scenarios.description: Different teams use different risk-scoring methodologies; severity scores not comparable across the register.
scenarios.remediation: Standardize on single methodology (e.g., 5x5 likelihood × impact matrix); train risk owners; re-score existing register.

scenarios.title: Residual risk acceptance lacks management signoff
scenarios.description: 30% of 'retain' risk-treatment decisions lack documented management signoff. Some retain decisions made by individual contributors.
scenarios.remediation: Define signoff matrix by severity; backfill where possible; route remaining retain decisions through proper authority.

scenarios.title: Risk register not updated for 6+ months
scenarios.description: Risk register last refreshed > 6 months ago. New risks from product changes, new vendors, regulatory developments not captured.
scenarios.remediation: Refresh; commit to quarterly cadence minimum.

scenarios.title: DPIA exists but Article 35(7) elements incomplete
scenarios.description: DPIA documented for high-risk processing but does not cover all Article 35(7)(a)-(d) required elements (missing necessity + proportionality assessment).
scenarios.remediation: Update DPIA template; refresh affected DPIAs.

scenarios.title: Risk treatment plans lack effective-date tracking
scenarios.description: Treatment plans are documented but lack effective-date or expected-completion fields; cannot track remediation timeliness.
scenarios.remediation: Add date fields; update existing entries.

scenarios.title: Consider FAIR quantitative risk methodology for top-tier risks
scenarios.description: Current methodology is qualitative; quantitative analysis (e.g., Open FAIR) for top-5 risks would improve decision quality.
scenarios.remediation: Pilot FAIR on 2-3 top risks.

scenarios.title: Risk-related KPIs not reported to executive
scenarios.description: Risk register exists but no rolled-up KPIs (e.g., # critical risks open, mean time to treatment) reported in management review.
scenarios.remediation: Add risk KPIs to management review inputs.

scenarios.title: Critical SaaS in use without DPA
scenarios.description: Critical SaaS supplier (handles personal data of 500K+ users) in use without signed DPA per GDPR Article 28. Pre-existing arrangement not refreshed since 2018.
scenarios.remediation: Engage vendor for DPA execution; if vendor refuses, evaluate replacement.

scenarios.title: Business Associate Agreement missing for HIPAA-relevant vendor
scenarios.description: Vendor processes PHI on behalf of the organization but no signed Business Associate Agreement (BAA) per HIPAA §164.314(a). Material exposure.
scenarios.remediation: Sign BAA; if vendor refuses, evaluate replacement; document remediation timeline.

scenarios.title: Annual supplier reviews incomplete
scenarios.description: Annual supplier security review not completed for 3 of 8 critical suppliers in past year.
scenarios.remediation: Run overdue reviews; calendar future reviews; document escalation for non-responsive vendors.

scenarios.title: Sub-processor list not maintained
scenarios.description: Critical supplier handling personal data uses sub-processors; the sub-processor list is not maintained or available; GDPR Article 28(2) not satisfied.
scenarios.remediation: Request sub-processor list from vendor; establish change notification mechanism; document.

scenarios.title: AI-specific contract clauses not in vendor agreements
scenarios.description: Third-party AI service in use; contract lacks AI-specific clauses (training-data use restrictions, drift notification, sub-processor list for AI sub-services).
scenarios.remediation: Negotiate addendum; document acceptance.

scenarios.title: Supplier exit / termination procedure not documented
scenarios.description: No procedure for safe vendor exit (data return, model deletion, monitoring transition). Discovered during attempt to terminate one supplier.
scenarios.remediation: Draft procedure; pilot on next vendor termination; document.

scenarios.title: Vendor onboarding checklist applied inconsistently
scenarios.description: Supplier onboarding checklist exists but is bypassed in 'urgent' procurements; 4 of 12 recent vendors lack complete onboarding evidence.
scenarios.remediation: Make checklist mandatory at procurement gate; remediate gaps in existing 4.

scenarios.title: Supplier SOC 2 reports collected but not reviewed
scenarios.description: Critical suppliers' SOC 2 Type II reports collected on initial onboarding but not reviewed annually as new reports issued.
scenarios.remediation: Set calendar for annual review; document key findings + acceptance.

scenarios.title: Consider centralizing supplier risk evidence in GRC tool
scenarios.description: Supplier evidence scattered across procurement Drive, Compliance Drive, and email. Centralization in GRC tool would reduce audit prep effort.
scenarios.remediation: Evaluate GRC tooling; migrate over 6 months.

scenarios.title: Vendor risk-tiering could be more granular
scenarios.description: Vendors tier as 'critical / non-critical' currently; more granular tiers (e.g., based on data type, criticality, integration depth) would refine review cadence.
scenarios.remediation: Define 3-tier model; reclassify existing inventory.

scenarios.title: GDPR Article 33 breach notification missed
scenarios.description: Breach occurred 96 hours ago; supervisory authority not notified despite Article 33 72-hour requirement. Investigation revealed unclear breach-criteria decision.
scenarios.remediation: File notification immediately with rationale for delay; review breach-criteria decision tree; conduct tabletop exercise; document.

scenarios.title: Recent P1 incident lacks PIR within SLA
scenarios.description: P1 production incident occurred 45 days ago; post-incident review (PIR) not documented within stated 30-day SLA.
scenarios.remediation: Complete PIR immediately; identify corrective actions; calendar future PIRs.

scenarios.title: Severity definitions inconsistently applied
scenarios.description: Severity definitions documented but inconsistently applied across teams; impact analysis varies. Two recent P2 incidents arguably P1 by definition.
scenarios.remediation: Train responders on severity rubric; calibration exercise quarterly; track severity-classification consistency.

scenarios.title: Notification SLAs not aligned across frameworks
scenarios.description: GDPR 72h, NIS2 24h-early-warning + 72h-notification, EU AI Act 15-day (or 2-day critical-infra), HIPAA 60-day. Internal procedures collapse to a single 'breach' notification without per-framework branching.
scenarios.remediation: Update IR procedure to branch by applicable framework; train responders.

scenarios.title: Incident commander rotation not documented
scenarios.description: Incident commander rotation exists informally but is not documented; recent incidents had ambiguous IC ownership.
scenarios.remediation: Document rotation; publish on-call schedule.

scenarios.title: Breach log incomplete per Article 33(5)
scenarios.description: GDPR breach log captures only DPA-notifiable events; Article 33(5) requires ALL breaches logged regardless of notifiability.
scenarios.remediation: Update breach log scope; backfill recent breaches; train DPO on requirement.

scenarios.title: Detection mechanism gaps
scenarios.description: Mean time to detect (MTTD) for past 3 incidents averaged 8 days; SIEM rules not tuned for recently-onboarded systems.
scenarios.remediation: Audit SIEM coverage; tune rules; test detection for high-impact attack scenarios.

scenarios.title: Tabletop exercise not conducted in last 12 months
scenarios.description: Annual incident-response tabletop exercise not performed in past 12 months.
scenarios.remediation: Schedule + run tabletop; document lessons learned.

scenarios.title: Customer notification timing not tracked
scenarios.description: Customer-facing incident notifications sent but timing not tracked against committed SLA. Cannot demonstrate SLA compliance.
scenarios.remediation: Track notification timestamps; report against SLA quarterly.

scenarios.title: Consider chaos engineering for resilience testing
scenarios.description: Incident response prepares for failures; chaos engineering would proactively surface latent weaknesses.
scenarios.remediation: Pilot chaos engineering on non-prod first; expand if mature.

scenarios.title: Production application logs disabled
scenarios.description: Production application logs disabled in past 30 days due to disk space; not detected until audit fieldwork. 30-day blind spot.
scenarios.remediation: Re-enable; resize storage; alert on log volume drops; investigate any incidents during blind period.

scenarios.title: Log retention misaligned with framework requirement
scenarios.description: Log retention configured at 90 days; ISO 27001 + framework requirements expect 12 months minimum for some logs.
scenarios.remediation: Update retention configuration; backfill from archives where feasible; document policy.

scenarios.title: Tamper-evident logging not enforced
scenarios.description: Tamper-evident logging not enforced on privileged-user activity logs; logs writable to same store as application data.
scenarios.remediation: Move logs to write-once storage; document architecture; verify immutability.

scenarios.title: AI model drift not monitored
scenarios.description: AI system in production; no drift monitoring against original validation data. No defined drift threshold for retraining.
scenarios.remediation: Implement drift monitoring; define threshold; escalation path.

scenarios.title: Monitoring alert thresholds not documented
scenarios.description: Monitoring alert thresholds exist in tooling but not documented; rationale unclear.
scenarios.remediation: Document thresholds + rationale + on-call response action.

scenarios.title: Cloud audit logs not centralized
scenarios.description: Cloud audit logs (CloudTrail/Cloud Audit Logs) exist per account but not centralized to SIEM; cross-account analysis manual.
scenarios.remediation: Forward logs to central SIEM; configure cross-account analysis.

scenarios.title: Consider anomaly detection on top of rule-based monitoring
scenarios.description: Current monitoring is rule-based; anomaly detection (statistical or ML-based) would surface novel patterns.
scenarios.remediation: Pilot on key data flows.

scenarios.title: Emergency change procedure not formalized
scenarios.description: Emergency change procedure not documented; observed 3 cases of production changes in past 30 days without recorded approval. Two affected customer data.
scenarios.remediation: Draft emergency change procedure including retroactive review; train engineers; audit recent emergency changes.

scenarios.title: Change advisory board rubber-stamps
scenarios.description: Change advisory board records show approvals but zero rejected changes in last 6 months. Board likely not exercising substantive review.
scenarios.remediation: Calibration training for board; track reject + revise rate; ensure reviewers have time + context.

scenarios.title: Rollback procedure not tested
scenarios.description: Rollback procedure documented but not tested for 2 services in audit scope. Cannot confirm operability.
scenarios.remediation: Test rollback in staging; document; schedule quarterly verification.

scenarios.title: Post-implementation reviews skipped for high-risk changes
scenarios.description: Change advisory board records show approvals but no post-implementation review for high-risk changes (defined by impact rubric).
scenarios.remediation: Reinstate post-implementation review for high-risk; define follow-up timeline.

scenarios.title: Link change records to deployment automation
scenarios.description: Change records and deployment automation are separate systems; linking would strengthen evidence chain.
scenarios.remediation: Integrate via deployment tagging.

scenarios.title: BCP/DRP exists but never tested
scenarios.description: Business continuity + disaster recovery plans exist on paper but no recovery exercise in 24+ months. Untested = ineffective.
scenarios.remediation: Conduct full DR exercise; document results; commit to annual exercise cadence.

scenarios.title: RPO/RTO objectives not measured
scenarios.description: Recovery objectives defined but not measured during recent failover events. Cannot confirm objectives are achievable.
scenarios.remediation: Measure during next exercise; tune objectives or recovery capability.

scenarios.title: Backup integrity not verified
scenarios.description: Backups occur but restoration testing not performed in past 12 months. Cannot confirm backups are usable.
scenarios.remediation: Quarterly restoration tests; document verification evidence.

scenarios.title: BCP doesn't address third-party SaaS outage
scenarios.description: BCP covers self-hosted infrastructure; doesn't address critical SaaS-vendor outage scenarios.
scenarios.remediation: Extend BCP for SaaS outage scenarios; document vendor SLAs + alternatives.

scenarios.title: Consider chaos game-day exercises
scenarios.description: Annual DR exercise meets standard; chaos game-day adds value by testing under more realistic conditions.
scenarios.remediation: Pilot game-day for one service.

scenarios.title: Annual security training not 100% complete
scenarios.description: Annual security training completion is 89% across the company; 12 employees past due > 30 days.
scenarios.remediation: Escalate to managers for non-completers; revoke access for chronic non-completers; document policy.

scenarios.title: AI literacy training not in place
scenarios.description: EU AI Act Article 4 requires AI literacy for staff dealing with AI systems; no AI-specific training implemented.
scenarios.remediation: Develop + roll out AI literacy training; track completion by role.

scenarios.title: Competence requirements undefined for ML engineers
scenarios.description: Competence requirements defined for engineering roles but not specifically for ML engineers; assumes 'they have degrees'.
scenarios.remediation: Define ML-engineer competence requirements; verify against existing staff.

scenarios.title: Training effectiveness verification missing
scenarios.description: Training completion recorded but effectiveness verification (assessment, simulation, observed behavior) not performed.
scenarios.remediation: Add post-training assessment; track scores.

scenarios.title: Consider role-based training tiers
scenarios.description: Training is uniform across roles; role-based tiers would surface compliance-officer-specific, dev-specific, etc.
scenarios.remediation: Design role-tiered curriculum.

scenarios.title: Training data lacks provenance records
scenarios.description: AI training data sourced from multiple vendors + scraped sources; no provenance records. EU AI Act Article 10(2)(d) + ISO 42001 A.7.4 not satisfied.
scenarios.remediation: Audit current training data; document provenance per source; remove data without verifiable provenance.

scenarios.title: PII in training data without lawful basis
scenarios.description: Training data contains PII; lawful basis (GDPR Article 6) not documented for AI training use case. Article 10(5) AI Act bias-detection exception not applicable here.
scenarios.remediation: Document lawful basis or remove PII; if legitimate interests, document LIA; halt training until resolved.

scenarios.title: Data quality dimensions not defined
scenarios.description: Data quality monitoring exists but dimensions (completeness, accuracy, timeliness, consistency) not formally defined. Audit against ISO 42001 A.7.3 incomplete.
scenarios.remediation: Define dimensions per data store; document measurement methodology.

scenarios.title: Article 30 RoPA stale
scenarios.description: GDPR Article 30 records of processing activities last refreshed 8 months ago; new processing activities not captured.
scenarios.remediation: Refresh RoPA; commit to quarterly updates; integrate with new-feature intake.

scenarios.title: Retention schedules not enforced
scenarios.description: Data retention schedules documented but not enforced in tooling. Data persists beyond stated retention.
scenarios.remediation: Implement automated retention enforcement; backfill cleanup; document deletions.

scenarios.title: Consent management workflow lacks withdrawal mechanism
scenarios.description: Consent collected at signup; withdrawal mechanism exists in privacy notice but not technically implemented.
scenarios.remediation: Implement self-service consent withdrawal; honour within reasonable time.

scenarios.title: Consider data lineage tooling
scenarios.description: Data flows documented manually; data-lineage tooling would automate + maintain freshness.
scenarios.remediation: Evaluate tooling (e.g., OpenLineage, DataHub, Atlan).

scenarios.title: Encryption at rest using deprecated algorithm
scenarios.description: Some data stores still use deprecated AES-128 (or 3DES); current standard expects AES-256.
scenarios.remediation: Plan migration; document; complete within 6 months.

scenarios.title: Key rotation not enforced
scenarios.description: Cryptographic key rotation policy exists (annual) but not enforced; production keys 3+ years old.
scenarios.remediation: Rotate immediately; automate rotation via KMS; document.

scenarios.title: TLS configuration permits deprecated versions
scenarios.description: TLS 1.0 + 1.1 still accepted on public endpoints; current standard expects TLS 1.2 minimum.
scenarios.remediation: Disable TLS 1.0 + 1.1; verify all clients support 1.2+; document.

scenarios.title: Cryptographic inventory incomplete
scenarios.description: Cryptographic inventory exists but lacks documentation of algorithm + key length per data store.
scenarios.remediation: Audit each store; document; flag deprecated algorithms.

scenarios.title: Consider post-quantum cryptography roadmap
scenarios.description: Current crypto is RSA + ECC; post-quantum standards finalized in 2024. Long-term planning for migration recommended.
scenarios.remediation: Define PQC migration roadmap.

scenarios.title: Production deploy without SAST results
scenarios.description: Recent production deploys lack SAST scan evidence; SAST configured in CI but bypassed via manual override.
scenarios.remediation: Make SAST a required gate; remove override capability for production; investigate bypassed deploys.

scenarios.title: Code review records inconsistent
scenarios.description: Some commits to main branch lack documented review; review-required branch protection not consistently enforced.
scenarios.remediation: Enforce review on protected branches across all repos; audit recent commits.

scenarios.title: Threat modeling not performed for new services
scenarios.description: New service launched last quarter without threat model. ISO 27001 A.8.25-31 + secure-by-design expectations not met.
scenarios.remediation: Retroactive threat model; integrate threat modeling into design-review gate.

scenarios.title: Dependency scanning missing for some repos
scenarios.description: Dependency scanning configured for production services but not for internal tools.
scenarios.remediation: Extend dependency scanning to all repos.

scenarios.title: Consider supply-chain security per SLSA
scenarios.description: Build provenance + supply-chain security gaps; SLSA framework would formalize improvements.
scenarios.remediation: Adopt SLSA Level 2 minimum for production builds.

scenarios.title: Critical vulnerabilities past patch SLA
scenarios.description: 5 critical-severity CVEs in production older than 30-day patch SLA; one is actively exploited in wild.
scenarios.remediation: Patch immediately; document compensating controls if patching not possible; investigate any compromise indicators.

scenarios.title: Vulnerability scanning not running weekly
scenarios.description: Scanning configured but execution stopped in past quarter due to tool change. 90+ day blind spot.
scenarios.remediation: Resume scanning; investigate vulnerabilities discovered post-resume.

scenarios.title: Patch SLAs not defined by severity
scenarios.description: Patch SLA defined for 'all CVEs within 90 days'; not differentiated by severity. Critical vulns should be < 30 days.
scenarios.remediation: Define severity-tiered SLAs; communicate; track compliance.

scenarios.title: Vulnerability exceptions lack expiry
scenarios.description: Exception tracking exists but exceptions have no expiry; some are 18+ months old without re-evaluation.
scenarios.remediation: Add expiry; re-evaluate all open exceptions.

scenarios.title: Consider container image base auditing
scenarios.description: Vulnerability scanning catches runtime; auditing base images at build time would prevent vulnerabilities reaching production.
scenarios.remediation: Add build-time scanning + base-image inventory.

scenarios.title: Server room access log incomplete
scenarios.description: Server room access log shows entries but lacks visitor escort records for 4 of 12 sampled entries.
scenarios.remediation: Reinforce escort policy; train + supervise; verify in next quarter.

scenarios.title: Workstation security policy not enforced
scenarios.description: Workstation locking policy documented but not enforced; observed several unattended unlocked workstations during walkthrough.
scenarios.remediation: Configure auto-lock at 5 min; train staff; verify.

scenarios.title: Visitor sign-in process bypassed
scenarios.description: Visitor sign-in book exists but bypassed for 'known' visitors; 8 sampled visits lack sign-in evidence.
scenarios.remediation: Reinforce policy + signage; consider electronic visitor management.

scenarios.title: Consider biometric access for sensitive zones
scenarios.description: Current access is card-based; biometric for sensitive zones (server rooms, R&D labs) would strengthen access discipline.
scenarios.remediation: Evaluate biometric tooling; pilot.

scenarios.title: Right to erasure not honored within SLA
scenarios.description: Erasure request from 60 days ago not fully completed; data persists in 3 systems including backups. GDPR Article 17 + 12(3) breached.
scenarios.remediation: Complete erasure; identify all systems; commit to per-system erasure workflow.

scenarios.title: International transfer without SCCs
scenarios.description: Personal data transferred to US subprocessor; no adequacy decision relied on, no SCCs signed, no derogation applies. Schrems II requirement breached.
scenarios.remediation: Execute SCCs (Commission 2021/914); conduct TIA per EDPB Rec. 01/2020; supplementary measures where needed.

scenarios.title: Privacy notice missing Article 13/14 elements
scenarios.description: Privacy notice published but lacks retention periods + data subject rights detail per Article 13(2).
scenarios.remediation: Update notice; publish version; track versions for evidence trail.

scenarios.title: Cookie banner pre-ticks consent
scenarios.description: Cookie banner pre-ticks non-essential cookies; valid consent per GDPR Article 7 + EDPB guidance requires affirmative action.
scenarios.remediation: Redesign banner; default to no consent for non-essential; document A/B test.

scenarios.title: DPO appointment not formal
scenarios.description: DPO exists but appointment letter not signed by senior management per GDPR Article 37 + 38. Reporting line ambiguous.
scenarios.remediation: Formal appointment letter; clarify reporting line to highest management; publish contact.

scenarios.title: DSAR identity verification process inconsistent
scenarios.description: DSAR identity verification varies across teams; one DSAR processed without proper identity check.
scenarios.remediation: Standardize verification procedure; train DPO + intake team.

scenarios.title: Consider privacy-enhancing technologies (PETs)
scenarios.description: Current privacy posture is procedural; PETs (differential privacy, federated learning, secure enclaves) for high-risk processing would reduce exposure.
scenarios.remediation: Pilot PET for one high-risk processing.

scenarios.title: Management review not performed in 18 months
scenarios.description: Management review last documented 18 months ago. Clause 9.3 expects at planned intervals (annual minimum). System effectiveness not formally evaluated.
scenarios.remediation: Schedule + conduct review; document inputs + outputs; calendar future reviews.

scenarios.title: Management review missing AI-specific inputs
scenarios.description: Management review covers ISMS but not AIMS-specific inputs (drift events, incidents, risk-register changes per ISO 42001 Clause 9.3).
scenarios.remediation: Update review template for AIMS inputs; include in next review.

scenarios.title: Open action items past due
scenarios.description: Management review action items: 4 of 9 past due > 60 days. Tracking not actively managed.
scenarios.remediation: Reassign owners; escalate stuck items; re-baseline due dates.

scenarios.title: Review attendance lacks senior leadership
scenarios.description: Review held but CEO + CTO absent; attendance of senior leadership expected per Clause 5.1 + 9.3.
scenarios.remediation: Schedule with leadership in advance; share inputs ahead of meeting.

scenarios.title: No internal audit programme
scenarios.description: Clause 9.2 internal audit programme not documented; audits happen ad-hoc; no rolling 3-year coverage plan.
scenarios.remediation: Design programme; assign auditors; schedule next 12 months minimum.

scenarios.title: Auditors audit own work
scenarios.description: Internal auditor for Clause 8.3 audit also owns the lifecycle process being audited. Independence breached.
scenarios.remediation: Reassign auditor; document independence verification per assignment.

scenarios.title: Audit findings not tracked to closure
scenarios.description: Audit findings logged but closure verification not consistently performed. 12 findings show 'closed' without evidence of effectiveness.
scenarios.remediation: Verify closure; require evidence; reopen unverified.

scenarios.title: Audit programme doesn't cover all clauses
scenarios.description: Audit programme covers Clauses 4-7 but not 8-10 in current 3-year cycle.
scenarios.remediation: Update programme; add missing clauses to remaining cycle.

scenarios.title: CAPA without effectiveness verification
scenarios.description: Corrective action plans documented + closed but effectiveness verification missing for 6 of 10 sampled CAPAs.
scenarios.remediation: Add measurable effectiveness verification to template; verify per CAPA; sample-check.

scenarios.title: Root cause analysis shallow
scenarios.description: Root cause analysis on CAPAs documented but stops at proximate cause (e.g., 'engineer made mistake'); 5 Whys not applied.
scenarios.remediation: Train CAPA owners on RCA methodology; re-do RCA on recent CAPAs.

scenarios.title: Trend analysis not performed
scenarios.description: Individual CAPAs handled but trend analysis across CAPAs not performed; missed systemic issues.
scenarios.remediation: Quarterly trend analysis; pattern identification; address systemic causes.

scenarios.title: Consider integrating CAPA into existing ticketing
scenarios.description: CAPA tracking in separate tool from incident tickets; integration would reduce overhead.
scenarios.remediation: Evaluate ticket-system extensions; pilot.

scenarios.title: Obsolete documents accessible
scenarios.description: Old versions of policies and procedures accessible in shared drives without 'obsolete' marking; risk of using superseded content.
scenarios.remediation: Archive obsolete versions; reorganize document drive; reinforce procedure.

scenarios.title: Document approval workflow bypassed
scenarios.description: Document approval workflow exists but 3 recent policy updates published without documented approval.
scenarios.remediation: Enforce workflow at publication; train owners; audit recent publications.

scenarios.title: Document review cadence not enforced
scenarios.description: Annual review cadence stated but 25% of controlled documents past due > 90 days.
scenarios.remediation: Calendar reviews; track due dates; remind owners.

scenarios.title: AI policy missing required commitments
scenarios.description: AI policy commits to lawful use only; missing beneficial purpose, human oversight, and continual improvement. ISO 42001 Clause 5.2 + Annex A.2.2 not satisfied.
scenarios.remediation: Rewrite policy with all 4 commitments; board signoff; publish.

scenarios.title: AIMS scope omits third-party AI
scenarios.description: AIMS scope statement (Clause 4.3) lists company-built AI systems but omits AI features in SaaS vendors used internally. Scope incomplete.
scenarios.remediation: Update scope; inventory third-party AI; include in AIMS controls.

scenarios.title: AI system lifecycle skips decommission
scenarios.description: AI lifecycle procedure (A.6) covers design through deployment + operation but lacks decommission phase. ISO 42001 expects full lifecycle.
scenarios.remediation: Define decommission procedure; train owners; document.

scenarios.title: V&V procedure for AI systems undefined
scenarios.description: Annex A.6.2.4 verification + validation procedure not documented; tests exist but acceptance criteria not formalized.
scenarios.remediation: Define V&V procedure; document acceptance criteria per system class; train.

scenarios.title: Impact assessment signed by wrong authority
scenarios.description: AI impact assessments for high-impact systems signed by tech lead; management approval expected per A.5.4.
scenarios.remediation: Define signoff authority by impact tier; re-route assessments; backfill where needed.

scenarios.title: Article 5 prohibited practice in production
scenarios.description: AI system performs emotion recognition in workplace setting; Article 5(1)(f) prohibition applies. System cannot remain on EU market.
scenarios.remediation: Disable in EU immediately; evaluate redesign for permitted use cases; document.

scenarios.title: High-risk AI without conformity assessment
scenarios.description: Annex III high-risk AI system on EU market; no Article 43 conformity assessment performed before placement.
scenarios.remediation: Withdraw from market until conformity assessment complete; document Annex IV; CE marking.

scenarios.title: Non-EU provider without authorized representative
scenarios.description: Non-EU provider placing AI system on EU market without appointed authorized representative per Article 22.
scenarios.remediation: Appoint EU-established authorized representative; document mandate.

scenarios.title: Article 50 transparency not implemented
scenarios.description: Customer-facing chatbot does not disclose AI interaction per Article 50(1).
scenarios.remediation: Add disclosure to UX; A/B test wording.

scenarios.title: GPAI without Article 53 technical documentation
scenarios.description: GPAI model provided to downstream integrators; Annex XI technical documentation not maintained.
scenarios.remediation: Develop documentation per Annex XI; publish training-data summary; copyright policy.

scenarios.title: DHF incomplete for commercial device
scenarios.description: Design history file for commercially distributed device lacks design validation evidence per ISO 13485 Clause 7.3.7.
scenarios.remediation: Compile validation evidence; document; if not feasible, withdraw + revalidate.

scenarios.title: Process validation stale
scenarios.description: Sterilization process not revalidated for 7 years despite supplier changes. ISO 13485 Clause 7.5.6 expects periodic revalidation.
scenarios.remediation: Revalidate; document; calendar future revalidation.

scenarios.title: Risk management file frozen at release
scenarios.description: ISO 14971 risk management file not updated post-launch; post-production information feedback not occurring.
scenarios.remediation: Update RMF with post-production information; commit to periodic review.

scenarios.title: PMCF plan exists but not executed
scenarios.description: Post-market clinical follow-up plan documented per EU MDR Annex XIV Part B; execution data lacking after 12 months.
scenarios.remediation: Execute per plan; document; report to notified body if outside plan.

scenarios.title: MDR-reportable event not reported
scenarios.description: Serious adverse event reportable per 21 CFR 803.50 not reported within 30 days. FDA enforcement exposure.
scenarios.remediation: File MDR immediately with delay rationale; review complaint trending; CAPA.

scenarios.title: Complaint files incomplete
scenarios.description: Complaint log per 21 CFR 820.198 missing investigation closure for 8 of 30 sampled complaints.
scenarios.remediation: Investigate + close; train complaint handlers.

scenarios.title: Form 483 open observations past response window
scenarios.description: Form 483 received 6 months ago; 2 of 5 observations lack documented response within 15-working-day window.
scenarios.remediation: Respond immediately; document corrective action; escalate to legal counsel.

scenarios.title: Labeling review evidence gaps
scenarios.description: Labeling per 21 CFR 801 reviewed at launch but no documented re-review for label changes in past 18 months.
scenarios.remediation: Audit labels; document review per change.

scenarios.title: PHI breach not assessed under Breach Notification Rule
scenarios.description: PHI exposure event 4 months ago; risk-of-compromise assessment per §164.402 not documented. Breach notification potentially required + missed.
scenarios.remediation: Conduct retroactive assessment; if breach, notify per §164.404 + §164.406; document.

scenarios.title: Security Risk Analysis not performed
scenarios.description: HIPAA Security Rule §164.308(a)(1)(ii)(A) risk analysis not documented in past 24 months despite material system changes.
scenarios.remediation: Conduct + document analysis; address top risks; calendar annual review.

scenarios.title: Encryption addressable spec not formally evaluated
scenarios.description: HIPAA encryption is 'addressable'; organization not encrypting PHI at rest in one data store; no documented analysis of why.
scenarios.remediation: Document analysis; if not encrypted, implement alternative protective measure or encrypt.

scenarios.title: Workforce sanctions policy not enforced
scenarios.description: §164.308(a)(1)(ii)(C) sanctions policy documented but no recorded sanctions despite repeat policy violations.
scenarios.remediation: Apply sanctions per policy; document; refresh training.

scenarios.title: Incident notification 24h early warning missed
scenarios.description: NIS2 Article 23 24-hour early warning to competent authority + CSIRT not provided after recent significant incident.
scenarios.remediation: File retrospectively; document delay rationale; engage authority; update IR procedure.

scenarios.title: Management body not approving cybersecurity measures
scenarios.description: NIS2 Article 20 requires management bodies to approve cybersecurity risk-management measures + oversee implementation. Approval missing from board minutes.
scenarios.remediation: Add to board agenda; document approval; ongoing oversight cadence.

scenarios.title: 10 minimum cybersecurity measures incomplete
scenarios.description: NIS2 Article 21(2)(a)-(j) 10 minimum measures: 2 not documented (policies on cryptography, basic cyber hygiene).
scenarios.remediation: Document missing policies; verify implementation; submit registration update.

scenarios.title: NIST CSF profile not defined
scenarios.description: Organization adopts NIST CSF 2.0 conceptually but no documented profile (current + target state) per CSF practice.
scenarios.remediation: Develop profile; identify gaps; roadmap.

scenarios.title: Recover function under-developed
scenarios.description: CSF GOVERN + IDENTIFY + PROTECT + DETECT + RESPOND well-developed; RECOVER function lacks documented recovery planning.
scenarios.remediation: Develop recovery planning + communications procedures.

scenarios.title: Service accounts without rotation
scenarios.description: Service-account credentials shared across systems; no rotation in past 24 months.
scenarios.remediation: Rotate; introduce secrets-management tooling; document.

scenarios.title: Privileged access logs not reviewed
scenarios.description: Privileged user activity logs collected but no periodic review for anomalous behavior.
scenarios.remediation: Define review cadence; assign reviewer; SIEM alerts for high-risk patterns.

scenarios.title: Break-glass account not monitored
scenarios.description: Emergency break-glass account exists but its usage not monitored; could be used without trace.
scenarios.remediation: Alert on break-glass usage; quarterly review.

scenarios.title: Personal device access not inventoried
scenarios.description: BYOD devices accessing corporate data not in asset inventory; mobile device management (MDM) coverage incomplete.
scenarios.remediation: Inventory BYOD; require MDM enrollment; document policy.

scenarios.title: Shadow IT discovered during audit
scenarios.description: 5 SaaS tools in use by teams without procurement / security review; some handle personal data.
scenarios.remediation: Bring shadow IT under management or sunset; revise procurement gate.

scenarios.title: Inventory not integrated with CMDB
scenarios.description: Asset inventory in spreadsheet; lacks integration with operational CMDB. Drift inevitable.
scenarios.remediation: Integrate via API or migrate to CMDB-as-source-of-truth.

scenarios.title: AI bias risk not formally identified
scenarios.description: AI risk register lacks systematic identification of bias risks across protected demographic categories.
scenarios.remediation: Apply ISO 23894 risk identification methodology; bias testing per category; document.

scenarios.title: Risk treatment costs not estimated
scenarios.description: Risk treatment plans don't estimate implementation cost; cost/benefit analysis missing.
scenarios.remediation: Add cost estimate field; quarterly review.

scenarios.title: Critical vendor SOC 2 expired
scenarios.description: Critical vendor's SOC 2 Type II report on file is 18 months old; current period not yet collected.
scenarios.remediation: Request current report; if vendor delayed, document compensating evidence.

scenarios.title: Vendor contact lists stale
scenarios.description: Vendor security contact information stale; recent contact attempts bounced.
scenarios.remediation: Refresh contact lists; verify quarterly.

scenarios.title: Forensic data preservation not standard
scenarios.description: Recent incidents lack forensic preservation of affected systems; impedes investigation.
scenarios.remediation: Document forensic preservation procedure; train IR team.

scenarios.title: External communications template missing
scenarios.description: External communications for incidents drafted ad-hoc; no pre-approved templates.
scenarios.remediation: Develop templates; legal + comms review; approve.

scenarios.title: Database query logging disabled
scenarios.description: Production database query logging disabled for performance reasons; can't audit who queried what.
scenarios.remediation: Enable query logging for sensitive tables; size storage; document trade-offs.

scenarios.title: Log timestamps not in standard timezone
scenarios.description: Logs across systems use mix of local timezones + UTC; correlation difficult.
scenarios.remediation: Standardize on UTC; document; backfill where feasible.

scenarios.title: Configuration drift not detected
scenarios.description: Production configuration drift from documented baseline; no detection mechanism.
scenarios.remediation: Deploy infrastructure-as-code drift detection; alert on deviations.

scenarios.title: Consider GitOps for change discipline
scenarios.description: Some changes still applied imperatively; GitOps would enforce change-via-PR discipline.
scenarios.remediation: Pilot GitOps for one infrastructure layer.

scenarios.title: Single region deployment without DR plan
scenarios.description: Production deployment in single AWS region; no documented multi-region or cross-region DR plan.
scenarios.remediation: Define DR plan (cross-region replicas, runbooks); test.

scenarios.title: Communications plan missing for major outage
scenarios.description: BCP covers technical recovery but lacks customer + employee communication plan for major outage.
scenarios.remediation: Develop communications plan; pre-approved templates; cascade.

scenarios.title: Onboarding security training not within 30 days
scenarios.description: Some new hires complete security training 60+ days after start; expected within 30 days.
scenarios.remediation: Calendar reminders; manager accountability; track completion timeline.

scenarios.title: Phishing simulation results trending up
scenarios.description: Phishing simulation click-rate increasing; training content may not be effective.
scenarios.remediation: Refresh training content; targeted training for repeat clickers.

scenarios.title: Data classification policy applied unevenly
scenarios.description: Data classification policy applied to engineering data stores but not marketing tools containing customer data.
scenarios.remediation: Extend classification; train marketing.

scenarios.title: Pseudonymization not consistently applied
scenarios.description: Pseudonymization documented for some pipelines; not consistently applied to analytics datasets containing personal data.
scenarios.remediation: Audit analytics datasets; pseudonymize where lawful basis is analytics.

scenarios.title: Keys stored alongside data
scenarios.description: Encryption keys stored in same cloud account / region as encrypted data; compromise of one yields the other.
scenarios.remediation: Move keys to dedicated KMS account; restrict access.

scenarios.title: Certificate expiration monitoring incomplete
scenarios.description: Certificate expiration alerts configured for some endpoints; internal certificates lack monitoring.
scenarios.remediation: Extend monitoring; centralize certificate inventory.

scenarios.title: Secrets in source control
scenarios.description: Code review uncovered API keys + DB credentials committed to git history.
scenarios.remediation: Rotate exposed secrets; remove from history; install pre-commit hooks; train.

scenarios.title: Pull-request templates lack security checklist
scenarios.description: PR templates exist but don't prompt security considerations (auth, input validation, secrets).
scenarios.remediation: Add security checklist to template; train.

scenarios.title: Penetration test recommendations untracked
scenarios.description: Annual penetration test completed; 12 findings; tracking + closure of remediation not centralized.
scenarios.remediation: Centralize tracking; assign owners; verify closure.

scenarios.title: Consider bug bounty programme
scenarios.description: External vulnerability discovery limited to annual pentest; bug bounty would broaden coverage.
scenarios.remediation: Evaluate bug bounty platforms; pilot.

scenarios.title: Clean desk policy not enforced
scenarios.description: Clean desk policy documented but walkthrough found sensitive printouts on unattended desks.
scenarios.remediation: Reinforce policy; periodic walkthroughs; train.

scenarios.title: Hardware disposal evidence incomplete
scenarios.description: Hardware disposal documented for laptops; lacks evidence of certified destruction for storage media.
scenarios.remediation: Use certified destruction service; collect certificates.

scenarios.title: DSAR response > 30 days
scenarios.description: 12 of 50 DSARs in past quarter responded after Article 12(3) 1-month SLA; no extension communicated.
scenarios.remediation: Investigate process bottlenecks; resource appropriately; communicate extensions where needed.

scenarios.title: Privacy notice version history missing
scenarios.description: Privacy notice updated multiple times; no version archive; cannot demonstrate which notice was active when.
scenarios.remediation: Archive past versions with date stamps.

scenarios.title: Article 22 automated decisions not flagged
scenarios.description: Automated decision-making (Article 22) used in credit decisions; data subjects not informed; human review not offered.
scenarios.remediation: Add transparency; offer human review; document procedure.

scenarios.title: Consider read-only published documents
scenarios.description: Controlled documents stored as editable Google Docs; risk of unauthorized edit. Read-only PDF publishing would be stronger control.
scenarios.remediation: Publish read-only PDFs; restrict editing to authors.

scenarios.title: Audit reports lack standard format
scenarios.description: Audit reports vary in format across auditors; difficult to compare or trend.
scenarios.remediation: Define standard report template.

scenarios.title: AI model card missing
scenarios.description: Production AI system lacks model card per Annex A.6.2.7. Documentation per Mitchell et al. (2019) pattern not produced.
scenarios.remediation: Develop model card; publish internally; commit to update with retraining.

scenarios.title: Datasheet for datasets not produced
scenarios.description: Training datasets lack datasheet per Gebru et al. (2021) pattern; not satisfying Annex A.7.4 fully.
scenarios.remediation: Develop datasheets per dataset; document provenance + composition + intended use.

scenarios.title: Article 27 FRIA missing for public-sector deployer
scenarios.description: Public-sector body deploying high-risk AI; Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment per Article 27 not performed.
scenarios.remediation: Conduct FRIA; document; consult DPA where required.

scenarios.title: EU database registration pending
scenarios.description: High-risk Annex III system not yet registered in EU database per Article 71.
scenarios.remediation: Register; document.

scenarios.title: Substantial modification turns deployer into provider
scenarios.description: Deployer substantially modified high-risk AI system; now operates as provider per Article 25(1) but did not assume provider obligations.
scenarios.remediation: Document role change; assume provider obligations; conformity assessment.

scenarios.title: Design transfer evidence missing
scenarios.description: Design transfer per Clause 7.3.8 not formally documented for recent product. Manufacturing operates with insufficient design records.
scenarios.remediation: Compile transfer evidence; document training; verify capability.

scenarios.title: Consider digital quality management system
scenarios.description: QMS run on shared drives; eQMS would improve traceability + audit-readiness.
scenarios.remediation: Evaluate eQMS vendors; pilot.

scenarios.title: UDI compliance gaps
scenarios.description: Some devices commercially distributed lack UDI labeling per 21 CFR 830.
scenarios.remediation: Audit + label; submit to GUDID; document.

scenarios.title: Pre-submission strategy could leverage Q-sub
scenarios.description: Product strategy proceeds toward 510(k) without leveraging FDA Q-Submission programme.
scenarios.remediation: Consider Q-sub for novel aspects.

scenarios.title: Workforce member access not minimum-necessary
scenarios.description: Workforce access provisioned at role level rather than minimum-necessary per §164.502(b). Some members access PHI beyond their need.
scenarios.remediation: Audit + tighten access; document minimum-necessary determination.

scenarios.title: Notice of privacy practices outdated
scenarios.description: Notice of privacy practices per §164.520 last updated 2 years ago; substantive policy changes not reflected.
scenarios.remediation: Update notice; redistribute per requirement; document.

scenarios.title: Registration with competent authority pending
scenarios.description: Organization meets NIS2 essential entity criteria but has not registered with national competent authority per Article 24.
scenarios.remediation: Submit registration; document.

scenarios.title: Supply-chain security measures not documented
scenarios.description: NIS2 Article 21(2)(d) supply-chain security measures not separately documented from generic supplier-management.
scenarios.remediation: Document NIS2-specific supply-chain measures.

scenarios.title: CSF tiers not assigned
scenarios.description: NIST CSF 2.0 implementation tiers (Partial / Risk Informed / Repeatable / Adaptive) not assigned per function.
scenarios.remediation: Self-assess tiers; document; target tier.

scenarios.title: EU MDR technical documentation gap
scenarios.description: Technical documentation per Annex II/III lacks recent clinical-evaluation update; notified body audit imminent.
scenarios.remediation: Update documentation immediately; engage notified body.

scenarios.title: Person Responsible for Regulatory Compliance not appointed
scenarios.description: EU MDR Article 15 PRRC role not formally appointed for the EU operations.
scenarios.remediation: Appoint PRRC meeting Article 15(1)-(2) qualifications; document.

scenarios.title: PMCF reports lag schedule
scenarios.description: Post-Market Clinical Follow-up reports not produced per agreed schedule.
scenarios.remediation: Catch up; rebaseline schedule.

scenarios.title: Risk management plan not updated for software change
scenarios.description: ISO 14971 risk management plan + risk file not updated after material software change.
scenarios.remediation: Update RMF; re-evaluate risks; document.

scenarios.title: Residual risk evaluation lacks acceptability criteria
scenarios.description: Residual risk evaluated but acceptability criteria per ISO 14971 §7 not formally established.
scenarios.remediation: Define acceptability criteria; document.

scenarios.title: EUDAMED registration incomplete
scenarios.description: EU MDR EUDAMED registration of device, manufacturer, or UDI elements incomplete despite mandatory data submission requirements.
scenarios.remediation: Complete required EUDAMED modules; track future module activations.

scenarios.title: Vigilance reporting log incomplete
scenarios.description: EU MDR vigilance reporting log per Article 87 has 3 entries past 15-day reporting timeline.
scenarios.remediation: Investigate root cause; tighten internal SLA; train.

scenarios.title: Production + post-production information feedback weak
scenarios.description: ISO 14971 §9 requires production + post-production information be collected + analysed; current process only acts on customer complaints, missing field data + service trends.
scenarios.remediation: Expand information sources; document process; integrate with PMS.

scenarios.title: Deepfake content not marked AI-generated
scenarios.description: Generative AI feature produces audio/video without machine-readable AI-generated marking per Article 50(2).
scenarios.remediation: Implement watermarking; document.

scenarios.title: Instructions for use missing operational risks section
scenarios.description: Article 13 instructions for use provided to deployers but do not adequately describe foreseeable operational risks.
scenarios.remediation: Update IFU with risks + mitigations; train downstream.

scenarios.title: Cybersecurity for connected device not addressed in 510(k)
scenarios.description: Connected device 510(k) submission lacks cybersecurity content per FDA Cybersecurity Guidance (Sep 2023); FDA refused acceptance.
scenarios.remediation: Develop cybersecurity content per guidance; resubmit.

scenarios.title: 510(k) summary lacks comparative data
scenarios.description: 510(k) summary per 21 CFR 807.92 lacks substantive comparison to predicate device.
scenarios.remediation: Add comparative data; resubmit if FDA requests.

scenarios.title: Workforce member termination workflow missing PHI access revocation
scenarios.description: Termination workflow revokes general access but doesn't specifically address PHI access systems; 2 terminated members retained EHR access > 2 days.
scenarios.remediation: Add PHI-specific revocation step; verify.

scenarios.title: Management review inputs not pre-distributed
scenarios.description: Management review held but inputs distributed only at meeting; senior leadership cannot prepare in advance.
scenarios.remediation: Pre-distribute inputs 1 week in advance.

scenarios.title: Audit programme could integrate cross-framework findings
scenarios.description: Audits performed per framework but cross-framework finding impact not systematically tracked; missed reuse opportunity.
scenarios.remediation: Use compliance-os cross_framework_mapper output to tag findings.
